marketState: neutral — bearish near-term technicals vs. bullish medium-term weather/positioning
summary: Front-month NG trades at $2.843, essentially at fair value ($2.923, band $2.423–$3.423) but running well below the 16-year seasonal median (-9.7pp) with low seasonal reliability this week. Near-term technicals are uniformly bearish across all six timeframes (negative MACD, 50<200 EMA cross on every horizon) and reinforced by a bearish storage surprise (+22 Bcf vs forecast, +180 Bcf vs 5y). The medium-term picture is different: 8-16 day demand is running +22.3% above the 5-year average near the top of its range, fair value drifts up toward $3.00 by day 21, and managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years — a crowded, contrarian setup vulnerable to short-covering. Net view: lean short into near-term technical/storage weakness while positioning for a medium-term recovery driven by hot weather, rising fair value, and squeeze risk in positioning.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D demand is +22.3% vs 5y at the top of its range (rangePos 0.90) and fair value is drifting up from $2.923 now to $3.000 by +21D while spot stays flat, so the fair-value gap should close higher into peak cooling season. Managed money net short at the 8th 5y percentile (ratio 0.68) is a crowded, contrarian setup that historically unwinds via short-covering.
longTerm.invalidation: Seasonal reliability is Low (59pp 10-90% spread) and probHigher-in-4-weeks is only 38%; a deepening storage surplus (already +180 Bcf vs 5y) or weather normalizing back toward the 5y average in the 8-16D window would invalidate the bullish weather thesis.

shortTerm.entry: $2.83 - $2.86 (sell into a bounce toward EMA resistance)
shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk (trim size — crowded-short COT raises squeeze risk)
shortTerm.rationale: All six technical timeframes (5m through 1w) show bearish trend, negative MACD histogram, and 50<200 EMA crosses, and this week's storage build beat forecast by +22 Bcf (bearish surprise). Near-term weather (1-7D) is only mildly above normal (+3.2% vs 5y, rangePos 0.56) so it is not yet a strong bullish counterweight, and the front-to-next futures spread is in slight backwardation (-$0.021), consistent with soft near-term positioning.
shortTerm.invalidation: RSI is already in the low-to-mid 30s on shorter timeframes (5m 31.6, 1d 34.5), leaving limited room before oversold conditions bring a bounce; a reversal of the EMA cross or a break back above $2.95 would invalidate the short.
